file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/1034.lean` ‚ÄĒ ErdŇĎs Problem 1034

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line (`import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`), immediately after the copyright block, separated by one blank line.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 1034`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1034`.
- [ ] **Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.**
  - **Issue:** The module docstring contains only the heading, reference link, and a bibliography entry (`[Er93]`). The actual plain-English problem description appears only in the theorem docstring. The checklist requires the module docstring itself to include a brief description.
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[Er93] ErdŇĎs, P., *On some of my favourite theorems* (1993), p. 344.`

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1034 ... end Erdos1034`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos1034`).
- [x] `open SimpleGraph Finset` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `adjToAtLeastTwoOfTriangle` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Marked `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
- [x] Function name uses `lowerCamelCase` (`adjToAtLeastTwoOfTriangle`).
- N/A: No `local notation` needed; no `@[category test/API]` sanity checks (reasonable given definition simplicity).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_1034` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) with problem description and solution status.
- [x] Docstring references the source paper: `[Er93, p. 344]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks a yes/no question: *"For every őĶ > 0, does there exist N‚āÄ such that ‚Ä¶?"* ‚ÄĒ this is **Case 1**.

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(False) ‚ÜĒ P`, matching Case 1 format.
- [x] `answer(False)` is correct: the problem was **disproved** (Ma and Tang constructed a counterexample), so the answer to the yes/no question is "no".
- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the question as originally asked (the positive claim), so `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` means "no".
- [x] Quantifiers (`‚ąÄ őĶ`, `‚ąÉ N‚āÄ`, `‚ąÄ n`, `‚ąÄ G`, `‚ąÉ u v w`) are **after** `answer(False)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Has exactly one `@[category research solved]`.
- [x] `research solved` is a valid category value (problem disproved by Ma and Tang, but not formally verified in Lean).
- [x] Contains `sorry` in proof body, which is consistent with `research solved` (not formally verified).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Has exactly one `@[AMS 5]` attribute (combinatorics).
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
- N/A: Only one number, so ordering and duplicates are not applicable.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` + newline + `  sorry` (acceptable multi-line format per checklist ¬ß9).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the conjecture.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. Single-part problem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `őĶ`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚Ąē`, `‚Č•`, `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚ąß`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines reasonably short (all < 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review.
- [ ] Custom linter checks not verified during this review.

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS with one minor issue**

The file is well-structured and conforms to the checklist on nearly all points. The formalization correctly identifies the problem as a yes/no question (Case 1), uses `answer(False)` since the conjecture was disproved, and places all quantifiers on the RHS. The custom definition is properly documented, marked `noncomputable`, and uses `lowerCamelCase`. Attributes are correctly formatted on a single line.

**Issue found:**

1. **¬ß3 ‚ÄĒ Module docstring missing plain-English description.** The module docstring has the heading, reference link, and bibliography entry, but no brief description of the problem. The problem description is only in the theorem docstring. Adding a one- or two-sentence summary to the module docstring (e.g., *"ErdŇĎs asked whether every dense graph contains a triangle whose vertices have many common neighbors."*) would satisfy this requirement.

content.text: The review passed with one minor issue: the module docstring is missing a plain-English description. Let me read the current file and fix it.

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1034
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1034](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1034)
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí[Er93] ErdŇĎs, P., *On some of my favourite theorems* (1993), p. 344.
    25‚Üí-/
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Finset
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üínamespace Erdos1034
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí/-- The number of vertices in a graph adjacent to at least two of three
    32‚Üígiven vertices $u$, $v$, $w$. -/
    33‚Üínoncomputable def adjToAtLeastTwoOfTriangle {n : ‚Ąē} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n))
    34‚Üí    (u v w : Fin n) : ‚Ąē :=
    35‚Üí  (Finset.univ.filter fun x : Fin n =>
    36‚Üí    (G.Adj x u ‚ąß G.Adj x v) ‚ą® (G.Adj x v ‚ąß G.Adj x w) ‚ą® (G.Adj x u ‚ąß G.Adj x w)).card
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/--
    39‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 1034** (Disproved) [Er93, p. 344]:
    40‚Üí
    41‚ÜíFor every $\varepsilon > 0$, does there exist $N_0$ such that for all $n \ge N_0$, every graph on
    42‚Üí$n$ vertices with more than $n^2 / 4$ edges contains a triangle $\{u, v, w\}$ such that
    43‚Üímore than $(1/2 - \varepsilon) \cdot n$ vertices are each adjacent to at least two of $u$, $v$, $w$?
    44‚Üí
    45‚ÜíDisproved by Ma and Tang, who construct a graph with $n$ vertices and
    46‚Üí$> n^2 / 4$ edges in which every triangle has at most $(2 - \sqrt{5/2} + o(1))n$
    47‚Üívertices adjacent to at least two of its vertices ($2 - \sqrt{5/2} \approx 0.4189$).
    48‚Üí-/
    49‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    50‚Üítheorem erdos_1034 : answer(False) ‚ÜĒ
    51‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    52‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    53‚Üí    ‚ąÄ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    54‚Üí      G.edgeFinset.card > n ^ 2 / 4 ‚Üí
    55‚Üí      ‚ąÉ u v w : Fin n, G.Adj u v ‚ąß G.Adj v w ‚ąß G.Adj u w ‚ąß
    56‚Üí        (adjToAtLeastTwoOfTriangle G u v w : ‚ĄĚ) > (1 / 2 - őĶ) * (n : ‚ĄĚ) := by

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 1034

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1034](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1034)

[Er93] ErdŇĎs, P., *On some of my favourite theorems* (1993), p. 344.
-/

open SimpleGraph Finset

namespace Erdos1034

/-- The number of vertices in a graph adjacent to at least two of three
given vertices $u$, $v$, $w$. -/
noncomputable def adjToAtLeastTwoOfTriangle {n : ‚Ąē} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n))
    (u v w : Fin n) : ‚Ąē :=
  (Finset.univ.filter fun x : Fin n =>
    (G.Adj x u ‚ąß G.Adj x v) ‚ą® (G.Adj x v ‚ąß G.Adj x w) ‚ą® (G.Adj x u ‚ąß G.Adj x w)).card

/--
**ErdŇĎs Problem 1034** (Disproved) [Er93, p. 344]:

For every $\varepsilon > 0$, does there exist $N_0$ such that for all $n \ge N_0$, every graph on
$n$ vertices with more than $n^2 / 4$ edges contains a triangle $\{u, v, w\}$ such that
more than $(1/2 - \varepsilon) \cdot n$ vertices are each adjacent to at least two of $u$, $v$, $w$?

Disproved by Ma and Tang, who construct a graph with $n$ vertices and
$> n^2 / 4$ edges in which every triangle has at most $(2 - \sqrt{5/2} + o(1))n$
vertices adjacent to at least two of its vertices ($2 - \sqrt{5/2} \approx 0.4189$).
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_1034 : answer(False) ‚ÜĒ
    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    ‚ąÄ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
      G.edgeFinset.card > n ^ 2 / 4 ‚Üí
      ‚ąÉ u v w : Fin n, G.Adj u v ‚ąß G.Adj v w ‚ąß G.Adj u w ‚ąß
        (adjToAtLeastTwoOfTriangle G u v w : ‚ĄĚ) > (1 / 2 - őĶ) * (n : ‚ĄĚ) := by
  sorry

end Erdos1034

input.old_string:
/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 1034

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1034](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1034)

[Er93] ErdŇĎs, P., *On some of my favourite theorems* (1993), p. 344.
-/
input.new_string:
/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 1034

ErdŇĎs asked whether every dense graph (with more than $n^2/4$ edges) contains a triangle
whose vertices have many common neighbors ‚ÄĒ specifically, more than $(1/2 - \varepsilon)n$
vertices each adjacent to at least two of the triangle's vertices.

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1034](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1034)

[Er93] ErdŇĎs, P., *On some of my favourite theorems* (1993), p. 344.
-/

content.content: The file /home/ryan/erdos/deepmind/1034.lean has been updated successfully.

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 1034

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1034](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1034)

[Er93] ErdŇĎs, P., *On some of my favourite theorems* (1993), p. 344.
-/

open SimpleGraph Finset

namespace Erdos1034

/-- The number of vertices in a graph adjacent to at least two of three
given vertices $u$, $v$, $w$. -/
noncomputable def adjToAtLeastTwoOfTriangle {n : ‚Ąē} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n))
    (u v w : Fin n) : ‚Ąē :=
  (Finset.univ.filter fun x : Fin n =>
    (G.Adj x u ‚ąß G.Adj x v) ‚ą® (G.Adj x v ‚ąß G.Adj x w) ‚ą® (G.Adj x u ‚ąß G.Adj x w)).card

/--
**ErdŇĎs Problem 1034** (Disproved) [Er93, p. 344]:

For every $\varepsilon > 0$, does there exist $N_0$ such that for all $n \ge N_0$, every graph on
$n$ vertices with more than $n^2 / 4$ edges contains a triangle $\{u, v, w\}$ such that
more than $(1/2 - \varepsilon) \cdot n$ vertices are each adjacent to at least two of $u$, $v$, $w$?

Disproved by Ma and Tang, who construct a graph with $n$ vertices and
$> n^2 / 4$ edges in which every triangle has at most $(2 - \sqrt{5/2} + o(1))n$
vertices adjacent to at least two of its vertices ($2 - \sqrt{5/2} \approx 0.4189$).
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_1034 : answer(False) ‚ÜĒ
    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    ‚ąÄ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
      G.edgeFinset.card > n ^ 2 / 4 ‚Üí
      ‚ąÉ u v w : Fin n, G.Adj u v ‚ąß G.Adj v w ‚ąß G.Adj u w ‚ąß
        (adjToAtLeastTwoOfTriangle G u v w : ‚ĄĚ) > (1 / 2 - őĶ) * (n : ‚ĄĚ) := by
  sorry

end Erdos1034
